message:
fix(export): add pre-flight validation, timeouts, and better error guidance

Export Pages:
- Add pre-flight validation to ensure parent directory exists before
  creating output directory, with clear error messaging
- Enhance error messages for preview server to guide users to run
  --export-pages first when bundle is missing

Preview Server:
- Add socket read/write timeouts (5s read, 10s write) in
  handle_preview_request() to prevent connection hangs

Beads:
- Rotate br_history snapshots (remove 2026-03-15, add 2026-03-19)
- Update issues.jsonl with current task state

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -99,7 +99,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-2bpl.4.3","title":"Re-audit workspace, hook, and repo-path semantics across all integration surfaces","description":"Purpose:
Many recently fixed bugs came from path-resolution assumptions. This audit must look for the same class of issue across exports, hooks, feedback/config files, workspace loading, watch paths, preview serving, and related operational flows.

Done means:
Path semantics are treated as a first-class parity surface with explicit evidence and follow-up tasks.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:39:46.639260766Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-19T03:43:35.164586654Z","closed_at":"2026-03-19T03:43:35.163844201Z","close_reason":"Audit complete. 10 remaining gaps documented after 5 concrete path-resolution bugs were fixed by prior agents during this audit lifecycle. Key remaining issues: hook stderr not captured, hook cwd may be wrong, no multiple .jsonl warning, git history not per-repo in workspace mode. Full findings in comment for bd-2bpl.4.4.3.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["hooks","parity","paths","workspace"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.4.3","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.1.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:40:33.596122596Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.4.3","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:39:46.639260766Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":145,"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.4.3","author":"BrightSpire","text":"Audited baseline/drift path semantics and fixed a real workspace-root mismatch.

Problem: --baseline-info, --save-baseline, --check-drift, and --robot-drift were still using raw --repo-path (or .) instead of the resolved load target/project directory. When --repo-path pointed inside a repo that should auto-promote to a workspace root via .bv/workspace.yaml, baseline files were read/written under the nested repo instead of the workspace root.

Fixes landed:
- src/main.rs now routes all four baseline/drift entry points through project_dir_for_load_target(&cli)
- workspace_discovery_start_points() now normalizes relative --repo-path values against current_dir before dedup/discovery, preventing false ambiguity between relative and absolute views of the same workspace root

Evidence/tests:
- added unit test: resolve_issue_load_target_discovers_workspace_from_relative_repo_path_without_ambiguity
- added integration test: baseline_commands_use_workspace_root_when_repo_path_discovers_workspace
- verification passed via rch: cargo test --test cli_model_validation baseline_commands_use_workspace_root_when_repo_path_discovers_workspace; cargo check --all-targets; cargo clippy --all-targets
- cargo fmt --check still fails repo-wide on unrelated pre-existing formatting drift outside this slice; touched files were rustfmt-formatted locally","created_at":"2026-03-15T03:28:39Z"},{"id":161,"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.4.3","author":"WhiteMink","text":"Taking a concrete follow-up under the workspace/path audit: --robot-orphans currently reloads git commits from raw --repo-path / . even though the shared history path already resolves repo root via project_dir_for_load_target() + resolve_repo_root(). In workspace auto-discovery mode that can narrow orphan analysis to the nested repo while issue/history loading is workspace-aware. I am patching that mismatch and adding a nested --repo-path workspace regression.","created_at":"2026-03-16T03:38:40Z"},{"id":162,"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.4.3","author":"WhiteMink","text":"Landed another concrete workspace/path parity fix in src/main.rs: --robot-orphans now reloads git commits via resolve_repo_root(&cli) instead of raw --repo-path / ., so nested --repo-path invocation that auto-discovers a workspace no longer narrows orphan analysis to the nested repo. Added integration coverage in tests/cli_model_validation.rs for nested repo-path workspace discovery plus workspace-root git history. Manual command-level sanity check shows total_commits=2 and candidate_files includes apps/web/src/orphan.js in that scenario. Standard rch cargo test/check attempts were intermittently blocked by worker /tmp ENOSPC during C dependency compilation; cargo clippy --all-targets did complete on another worker with the repo’s existing warning-only output.","created_at":"2026-03-16T03:43:42Z"},{"id":164,"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.4.3","author":"ubuntu","text":"Added another workspace/path fix in src/main.rs: relative history/snapshot references now prefer the resolved project/workspace root before caller cwd shadow files. This closes a class of `--diff-since snapshots/...` bugs when invoking from outside the repo with --repo-path pointing into a workspace-discovered project. Added integration coverage in tests/cli_model_validation.rs for a caller-cwd shadow snapshot versus the intended workspace snapshot. Verification: rch cargo clippy --all-targets passed on vmi1264463 with the repo's existing warning-only output; rch cargo test --test cli_model_validation robot_diff_snapshot_prefers_workspace_relative_file_over_caller_cwd_shadow -- --exact and rch cargo check --all-targets are still active behind a worker-local build lock, so those two are not yet confirmed complete.","created_at":"2026-03-16T04:01:15Z"},{"id":198,"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.4.3","author":"ubuntu","text":"Progress update (BronzeGate): found and fixed a concrete workspace/path semantics mismatch in the export surfaces. Problem: when nested --repo-path auto-discovered and promoted to a workspace root, persisted-state paths and hook lookup already used the resolved project/workspace root, but relative --export-pages / --export-md targets still wrote under the caller cwd. Fix: src/export_md.rs now resolves relative export targets against the resolved project/workspace root before both hook env construction and the export callback, and src/main.rs pages export callsites now use the resolved export path. Added regressions in tests/export_md.rs and tests/export_pages.rs proving relative export paths under nested --repo-path workspace discovery land in the workspace root, not the caller cwd, and that BV_EXPORT_PATH matches the resolved target. Verification via rch: cargo test --test export_md export_md_relative_export_path_uses_workspace_root_when_repo_path_discovers_workspace -- --exact; cargo test --test export_pages export_pages_relative_export_path_uses_workspace_root_when_repo_path_discovers_workspace -- --exact; cargo check --all-targets; cargo clippy --all-targets. cargo fmt --check still fails repo-wide on unrelated pre-existing formatting drift outside this slice.","created_at":"2026-03-18T01:53:54Z"},{"id":199,"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.4.3","author":"SapphireLeopard","text":"Progress update (SapphireLeopard): fixed a concrete workspace/path mismatch in the preview surfaces. Problem: relative --preview-pages bundle paths were still resolved from caller cwd, and the interactive --pages wizard inherited the same issue when previewing a saved relative output_path, even when nested --repo-path auto-discovered a workspace root. Fix: src/main.rs now resolves preview bundle paths against the resolved project/workspace root before starting the preview server, and the wizard preview callback uses the same resolver. Added unit coverage in src/main.rs and integration coverage in tests/export_pages.rs. Verification: export TMPDIR=/data/tmp && rch exec -- cargo check --all-targets passed; export TMPDIR=/data/tmp && rch exec -- cargo clippy --all-targets passed with existing repo warning backlog only; export TMPDIR=/data/tmp && rch exec -- cargo fmt --check passed. Targeted unit test resolve_cli_path_from_project_dir_uses_workspace_root_when_repo_path_discovers_workspace passed; direct end-to-end runtime reproduction confirmed target/debug/bvr --preview-pages bundle --repo-path <nested repo> serves the workspace-root bundle when invoked from outside the workspace.","created_at":"2026-03-18T06:01:36Z"},{"id":218,"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.4.3","author":"FuchsiaAnchor","text":"## Workspace/Hook/Repo-Path Audit — FuchsiaAnchor (2026-03-19)

### Methodology
Code-level audit of src/loader.rs (workspace discovery, beads file resolution), src/cli.rs (path flags), src/export_md.rs (hooks), src/main.rs (path resolution), and integration surfaces. Builds on prior fix work by BrightSpire, WhiteMink, BronzeGate, SapphireLeopard.

### ALREADY FIXED (by prior agents during this audit lifecycle)
- Baseline/drift paths now use project_dir_for_load_target, not raw --repo-path (BrightSpire)
- --robot-orphans now uses resolve_repo_root for workspace-aware git loading (WhiteMink)
- Relative history/snapshot refs prefer workspace root over caller cwd (ubuntu)
- Relative --export-pages/--export-md paths resolve against workspace root (BronzeGate)
- Relative --preview-pages paths resolve against workspace root (SapphireLeopard)

### REMAINING GAPS

**W1. Symlink/canonicalization fragility in deduplication.** Workspace repo dedup uses canonicalized paths, but canonicalize() can fail on NFS/FUSE/broken symlinks, falling back to normalization that may not catch duplicates. No test coverage for symlink scenarios. (src/loader.rs)

**W2. Git worktree resolution fragile.** Worktree support reads .git file → commondir pointer → falls back to parent .git. Assumes commondir is 2 levels up from worktree root. Could break with unusual worktree layouts or moved main worktrees. (src/loader.rs)

**W3. Hook stderr not captured or displayed.** Pre/post-export hooks run via Command with stderr set to Stdio::piped() but stderr output is not read or logged. On hook failure, operator gets exit-code only, not the error message. (src/export_md.rs hook execution)

**W4. No warning when multiple preferred .jsonl files exist.** If both beads.jsonl and issues.jsonl exist in .beads/, loader silently picks beads.jsonl. Operator may not realize which file is being used. (src/loader.rs discovery chain)

**W5. Ancestor search stops at first .beads/ match.** If .beads/ exists at both project-root and parent-root, the first match wins. No validation that the found .beads/ is the intended one. (src/loader.rs)

**W6. Hook command runs in current_dir, not project_dir.** Hook env sets BV_PROJECT_DIR but the hook process itself may execute with cwd as caller's directory, not project directory. (src/export_md.rs)

**W7. No per-repo breakdown in robot output.** --robot-triage, --robot-history etc. aggregate across workspace repos but don't break down results by source_repo. Operators can't distinguish which repo contributes what. (src/main.rs, src/robot.rs)

**W8. No per-repo export option.** Export always bundles all workspace repos into one artifact. No flag to export individual repos from a workspace. (src/export_pages.rs)

**W9. History assumes single .git root.** Git history loading uses one git root for all commits. In workspace mode with multiple repos, each repo's git history should be loaded independently. (analysis/git_history.rs)

**W10. No test coverage for deep nesting or cross-mount paths.** Workspace tests cover basic 2-repo aggregation and nested --repo-path promotion, but no tests for deeply nested repos, symlinked repos, or cross-filesystem paths.

### STRONG POINTS
- Discovery chain (--beads-file > --workspace > auto > --repo-path) is well-documented and enforced
- Cross-repo dependency prefixing works correctly (repo:id format)
- Workspace validation catches duplicates, prefix collisions, empty repos
- Recent fixes have materially improved path resolution correctness across baseline/drift, orphans, export, and preview surfaces
- Integration test coverage for workspace discovery scenarios is solid

### RECOMMENDATIONS FOR bd-2bpl.4.4.3

Priority order:
1. W3 (hook stderr capture) — most operator-visible, easy fix
2. W6 (hook cwd) — correctness issue, may cause hook failures silently
3. W4 (multiple .jsonl warning) — prevents confusion
4. W9 (per-repo git history) — workspace correctness
5. W1 (symlink dedup) — edge case but can cause duplicate loading
","created_at":"2026-03-19T03:43:24Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2bpl.4.4","title":"Implement the highest-value integration fixes found by export/pages/workspace audit","description":"Purpose:
Implementation bucket for concrete export/pages/workspace mismatches uncovered by the fresh audit. Use this bead to track real code fixes, not speculative improvements.

Acceptance:
Each landed fix includes targeted regression tests and preserves deterministic operator behavior under explicit `--repo-path`, workspace-root, and non-repo cwd invocation scenarios.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:39:46.990920814Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-14T02:48:52.702204761Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["export","implementation","parity"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.4.4","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:39:46.990920814Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.4.4","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.4.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:40:34.305098979Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.4.4","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.4.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:40:35.087430954Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.4.4","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.4.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:40:35.773187319Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-2bpl.4.4.1","title":"Fix static export bundle and viewer-runtime mismatches surfaced by the integration audit","description":"Purpose:
Implement concrete fixes from `bd-2bpl.4.1` for export artifact parity: asset inventory, bootstrap/runtime payloads, offline/static-host assumptions, CSP/cache semantics, and viewer startup behavior.

Acceptance:
- Exported bundles behave correctly in the environments legacy users rely on.
- No existing export capability is removed or weakened.
- Root causes and proof obligations are recorded clearly.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:48:15.353993659Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-14T02:49:36.551075321Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["bundle","export","implementation","parity"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.4.4.1","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.4.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:49:36.550288954Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.4.4.1","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.4.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:48:15.353993659Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"bd-2bpl.4.4.2","title":"Fix preview, watch, and pages-wizard workflow mismatches surfaced by the integration audit","description":"Purpose:

 {"id":"bd-2bpl.4.4.3","title":"Fix workspace, hook, and repo-path mismatches surfaced by the integration audit","description":"Purpose:
Implement concrete fixes from `bd-2bpl.4.3` for path-rooted behavior across hooks, preview serving, watch loops, config/feedback storage, and workspace/repo-path resolution.

Acceptance:
- Invocation from non-repo cwd, workspace root, nested repo paths, and explicit file paths behaves predictably.
- Path-root assumptions are made explicit in code and tests.
- Fixes strengthen reliability without reducing capability.","status":"in_progress","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:48:16.038062797Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-19T03:47:14.111439210Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["hooks","implementation","parity","paths","workspace"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.4.4.3","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.4.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:49:37.864774342Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.4.4.3","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.4.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:48:16.038062797Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":219,"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.4.4.3","author":"FuchsiaAnchor","text":"FuchsiaAnchor: Implemented W4 fix from audit — find_jsonl_path() now logs a tracing::warn when multiple preferred JSONL files exist in .beads/ (e.g., beads.jsonl + issues.jsonl), showing which file was chosen and which were ignored. Previously this was silent, potentially confusing operators who didn't realize which source was being used. Added regression test for 3-file discovery scenario. Note: W3 (hook stderr) and W6 (hook cwd) from audit were false positives — code already handles both correctly.","created_at":"2026-03-19T03:47:14Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2bpl.4.5","title":"Expand shell-e2e and integration evidence for the audited integration workflows","description":"Purpose:
Strengthen the proof layer for export and workspace flows. This includes shell e2e for operator journeys and targeted integration coverage for failure paths, reload loops, and repo-path/workspace-resolution semantics.

Done means:
The most failure-prone integration workflows have durable evidence instead of one-off manual validation.","design":"Testing ladder:
1. Unit tests for pure export/watch/path semantics.
2. Integration tests for preview/watch/wizard and repo/workspace resolution behavior.
3. Shell e2e scripts for operator journeys with lifecycle/watch logging, preview endpoint evidence, and replay commands.

Path-resolution context must always be explicit in assertions and logging.","acceptance_criteria":"- Reopened integration gaps are covered by unit, integration, and shell-e2e layers where relevant.
- Logs identify cwd/repo/workspace assumptions and workflow stage on failure.
- Artifact capture is detailed enough to reproduce export/pages regressions quickly.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:39:47.363638807Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-14T02:49:50.714191703Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["export","parity","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.4.5","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:39:47.363638807Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.4.5","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.4.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:40:36.537585963Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":133,"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.4.5","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Planning refinement:
The proof obligation here is intentionally layered: unit + integration + shell-e2e with detailed logging. Do not close this track on a few narrow tests or a manually verified happy path.","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:49:50Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2bpl.4.5.1","title":"Add export/pages/workspace unit and integration regressions for reopened gaps","description":"Purpose:
Convert reopened integration gaps into durable proof with unit and integration tests. Cover bundle/runtime semantics, preview/watch internals, repo/workspace path resolution, and failure-path behavior.

Logging requirement:
Tests must identify which workflow stage and path-resolution context regressed.

Acceptance:
- Each reopened integration bug class has stable regression coverage.
- Tests are explicit about cwd/repo/workspace assumptions.
- Failure messages are useful without manual reproduction.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:48:16.361955207Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-14T02:49:39.995709443Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["export","integration","parity","testing","unit"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.4.5.1","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.4.4.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:49:38.550784681Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.4.5.1","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.4.4.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:49:39.294868863Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.4.5.1","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.4.4.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:49:39.994643378Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.4.5.1","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.4.5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:48:16.361955207Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
